name: Baseline-008: embedding-similarity detector (sentence-transformer cosine)
description: First non-trivial detection submission tested under v2 bars (D1+D2+D3). Scores cosine similarity between sentence-transformer (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) embeddings of (question, response). Higher similarity = more misconception-like under the hypothesis that misconception responses (full council restatements) share more semantic content with the question than terse truth responses.
pre_stated_prediction: submissions/baseline_008_embedding_similarity/PRE_STATED_PREDICTION.md committed at commit a05c8c1 BEFORE the gauntlet run. Predicted ~15-25% clear D3, ~60% pass D1+D2 fail D3, ~15% fail D1/D2. Actual outcome falls in the ~60% modal-prediction region.
notes: n=1 evidence that sentence-transformer cosine similarity is structurally equivalent to length on the D1 (broad misconception-vs-truth) partition (D1=0.805 vs length=0.790, delta 0.015 below D3's 0.10 threshold). On D2 (folklore-subset-vs-truth) the embedding adds 12.4 percentage points above length — real signal on that narrower axis but not enough to pass D3 jointly. The dark core stays dark to embedding-similarity detection at this corpus. A future submission with explicit length-residualization, specificity-detection, or NLI-with-answer-type-awareness might clear D3; the bar is doing its job by requiring genuinely-escape-the-artifact signal, not just smarter-encoded-length.
